elements.title: Nazi concentration camps
elements.contents: ... that there were more than a thousand Nazi concentration camps (prisoners pictured)?

elements.title: Thomas Johannes Mayer
elements.contents: ... that Thomas Johannes Mayer went from performing jazz, rock, and punk, to portraying operatic characters such as Wotan and Wozzeck?

elements.title: a 1948 yakuza film
elements.contents: ... that a 1948 yakuza film was censored when its original ending depicted one character driving the corpse of another around a black market?

elements.title: House of Balloons
elements.contents: ... that the Weeknd's album House of Balloons is named after a real house in which he used to host parties?

elements.title: Findley State Park
elements.contents: ... that an Ohio state judge's donation of land for Findley State Park was described as his "personal weapon in a long fight against juvenile delinquency"?

elements.title: Ingrid Fischbach
elements.contents: ... that Ingrid Fischbach did not expect to win her first election, instead considering it a "test run"?

elements.title: Wolsung
elements.contents: ... that one writer described the game Wolsung as "legendary" among the Polish role-playing community for its prolonged development?

elements.title: Mason Howell
elements.contents: ... that when 2025 U.S. Amateur champion Mason Howell was four, he dressed as golfer Bobby Jones for Halloween?

elements.title: Real Madrid's 1927 tour of the Americas
elements.contents: ... that, during Real Madrid's 1927 tour of the Americas, José María Muñagorri nearly jumped into shark-infested waters to rescue a teammate?
